name: Measurabl ESG Data Management API
what_it_does: Measurabl ESG data management REST API for commercial real estate owners, REITs, and property managers to collect, analyze, and report environmental, social, and governance performance data for sustainability reporting and GRESB benchmarking. Enables AI agents to manage utility data collection and energy consumption tracking for real estate energy management automation, handle carbon emission calculation and Scope 1/2/3 reporting for property carbon accounting automation, access water usage and waste management tracking for environmental performance automation, retrieve GRESB score and benchmark preparation for real estate sustainability reporting automation, manage ESG data collection from property managers and tenants for portfolio sustainability automation, handle green building certification tracking (LEED, BREEAM, ENERGY STAR) for sustainability credential management automation, access climate risk and physical risk assessment for real estate climate exposure automation, retrieve portfolio-level ESG dashboard and analytics for investor sustainability reporting automation, manage sustainability goal and target tracking for net-zero real estate pathway automation, and integrate Measurabl with utility billing, property management, and sustainability reporting frameworks for end-to-end real estate ESG management.
use_cases: Collecting utility data from real estate energy management automation agents
Calculating carbon emissions from property sustainability reporting agents
Preparing GRESB scores from real estate ESG benchmarking agents
Tracking net-zero targets from commercial real estate sustainability agents
not_for: Corporate enterprise-wide Scope 3 carbon accounting (use Watershed or Persefoni)
Manufacturing facility energy management (use Schneider Electric EcoStruxure)
best_when: A commercial real estate owner, REIT, institutional investor, or property manager wanting AI agents to automate ESG data collection, carbon emission calculation, GRESB preparation, and sustainability reporting across a commercial real estate portfolio.
avoid_when: UTILITY DATA AUTHORIZATION FOR TENANT SPACES: Automated utility data collection via Measurabl from tenant-controlled spaces requires tenant authorization; automated collection of tenant-submetered utility data without tenant consent creates data privacy exposure and potential lease dispute. CARBON EMISSION FACTOR VINTAGE AND METHODOLOGY: Automated carbon calculation via Measurabl must use current-year emission factors; outdated emission factors create carbon accounting inaccuracy in ESG disclosures; verify Measurabl emission factor vintage and update schedule for alignment with GHG Protocol reporting requirements. GRESB SUBMISSION DATA ACCURACY: Automated GRESB submission data prepared via Measurabl must be reviewed before submission; automated GRESB data with uncorrected data quality issues creates peer comparison distortion and potential GRESB score dispute; implement data quality review gate before automated GRESB submission.

auth.notes: Measurabl uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. San Diego, California HQ. Founded 2013 by Matt Ellis. Private (~$100M raised, JLL, CBRE, Crow Holdings). ESG platform for commercial real estate. GRESB official data partner. ENERGY STAR integration. Green building certification tracking. Portfolio benchmarking. Used by 700+ clients across 50,000+ buildings. Competes with Arcadis, Yardi Energy, and IBM TRIRIGA for real estate ESG data management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-asset annual subscription for portfolio ESG management
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-asset annual subscription based on building count; enterprise pricing for large REIT portfolios; GRESB reporting module included
pricing.notes: San Diego CA. Private (~$100M raised). Founded 2013. Per-asset subscription. Portfolio-size-based pricing. GRESB data partner. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; asset and utility queries throttled; webhooks for data ingestion and report events

known_agent_gotchas: UTILITY DATA COMPLETENESS AFFECTS GRESB SCORE: Automated GRESB data preparation via Measurabl requires complete utility coverage; missing utility data for partial building coverage creates GRESB score penalty for data completeness; automated data collection must target 100% building coverage before GRESB submission window; implement coverage tracking for automated pre-GRESB data completeness check

EMISSION FACTOR ANNUAL UPDATE REQUIRED: Measurabl carbon emission factors are updated annually; automated carbon calculation workflows must use current-year emission factor vintage; automated Scope 2 calculation using market-based emission factors requires renewable energy certificate (REC) purchase documentation upload before factor application

TENANT AUTHORIZATION FOR GREEN LEASE DATA SHARING: Automated utility data collection for tenant-controlled spaces requires green lease clause or tenant authorization; automated data collection from tenant-metered spaces without authorization creates lease dispute; implement tenant authorization confirmation before automated utility data collection from non-landlord-controlled meters

GRESB SUBMISSION WINDOW DEADLINE MANAGEMENT: GRESB data collection window is annual (April-July); automated GRESB data preparation must complete data collection, validation, and review before GRESB submission deadline; automated workflow that misses annual GRESB submission window requires waiting 12 months for next cycle; build submission deadline awareness into automated GRESB preparation workflow

ENERGY STAR PORTFOLIO MANAGER API CREDENTIAL ROTATION: Measurabl integrates with ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager via web services; automated ENERGY STAR data synchronization requires current Portfolio Manager API credentials; credential rotation in Portfolio Manager without updating Measurabl integration creates ENERGY STAR data sync failure for automated benchmarking workflows

CLIMATE RISK SCENARIO METHODOLOGY DISCLOSURE: Automated climate physical risk assessment via Measurabl uses climate scenario model assumptions (RCP 4.5, RCP 8.5); automated TCFD climate disclosure must document Measurabl's climate scenario methodology; undisclosed scenario assumption creates TCFD reporting transparency gap for institutional investor audiences
agent_readiness.security_notes: Real estate ESG management. SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Utility consumption data, carbon emissions, and property sustainability records.

security_score_components.security_notes: Real estate ESG. SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Utility consumption, carbon emissions, and sustainability data.
